response.justification: Candidate has strong general software engineering credentials and broad polyglot experience, but lacks the core requirements for this role: substantial recent C++ development on Windows, SQL Server/Oracle expertise, and fixed income or capital markets domain knowledge. The only C/C++ experience is a 7-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago (2014-2015), far short of the required 5-10 years. No financial services background is evident, and recent work is focused on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and AI tooling rather than capital markets systems.
strengths: Solid 10+ year software engineering track record across diverse domains
Strong testing and software craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, pairing)
Polyglot programmer comfortable picking the right tool for the job
concerns: Only ~7 months of C/C++ experience (2014-2015), far below the 5-10 year requirement
No fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domain experience
No demonstrated SQL Server/Oracle or Windows-platform development background

key_factors.factor: No fixed income / capital markets domain exposure
Strong general engineering fundamentals and testing discipline
